name: 1 Bdrm Apt With Air-Con & Balcony In Melb CBD
description:
Summary:
Located in Melbourne’s CBD (Downtown), this stylish 1 bedroom,1 bathroom apartment features floor-to-ceiling windows with stunning city views from its living area and bedroom, wall-mounted air-con and a balcony with a table for 2. The building complex itself offers a gym, swimming pool and 2 tennis courts. Southern Cross Station and Marvel Stadium are close by, as well as the Crown Complex and Casino, Exhibition Centre and Aquarium, with trams outside the building free within the CBD

The space
The space:
Enter this beautifully designed 1 bedroom, 1 bathroom apartment into an open plan living/dining/kitchen space with stylish floorboards throughout. The kitchen comes equipped with double sink, 4 burner gas cooktop, under-bench electric oven, microwave, Nespresso coffee maker and separate milk frother, while the dining area has a table for 4 people and flows through to the living space with a smart TV, occasional chair, floor rug and a 2-sea
neighborhood_overview:
The neighbourhood:
Located in the heart of Melbourne, you’ll enjoy all the conveniences that the CBD has to offer. Bourke Street Mall is just a stroll away, home to many major shopping retailers and close to countless shops and eateries in the surrounding streets and laneways that Melbourne is famous for. Marvel Stadium is less than 10 minutes walk away, with Southbank shopping/restaurant precinct just across the Yarra River. Federation Square, the National Gallery of Victoria, Melbourne’s Chinatown, Crown Casino and Flagstaff Gardens are also close by.
host_about:
Hey I’m Mark!

After an extensive, colorful career performing on stage and in film/TV, I developed people skills which I love to utilize as a property host. People consider me to be a genuinely warm, polite and friendly person. 

I certainly love helping travelers enjoy their time here. My yardstick is treating
guests the way I like to be treated when I’m away from home. I work for MaisonNets, a boutique agency specializing in short-term rental management. The MaisonNets portfolio has over 125 properties, encompassing mid-range to luxury apartments and houses in Australia’s capital cities, as well as country homes and a villa in Bali. 

Our properties are all specifically selected for their amenities and prime location - the accommodation of choice for urban sophisticates, professionals, couples and families looking for a uniquely comfortable experience. 

With a focus on organic products, MaisonNets offers unparalleled personal service and attention to detail ensuring that you, our valued guest, have a wonderful stay with us.

description:
This is a spacious and light-filled 1 bedroom apartment in an original warehouse building in one of Melbourne’s popular inner north areas. It is part of the main residence, both share the same front secure gate with its own entry door. It is furnished, secure and self-contained. The original warehouse was a shoe factory and many of the original external walls and features have been retained to give a genuine warehouse character to the apartment.

The space
For reviews please see our similar listing - 1 Bedroom Warehouse Studio Apartment which is the same space.

The two large windows in the living area over look the street. Facing north they provide an abundance of light throughout the day and create a great connection with the street. The changing light throughout the day gives it a wonderful charm and offers a comfortable space that feels like a home.

The main residence in the warehouse is our home.

Pretty Elwood with its Art Deco charm and village-like feel, personifies easy, breezy Bayside living. Should things ever feel too sleepy, the unrelenting buzz of St Kilda is just around the corner.

It can’t be denied – Elwood is a good-looking suburb. From the sunny, toned Art Deco apartments that line its streets to the ample parks and beachfront promenade, this neighbourhood is white-picket fence lovely. With easy access to the city and the ocean – it offers residents a charmed lifestyle by the bay.

Leafy, community-oriented and very liveable, Elwood is a tightly-knit neighbourhood. Nearby St Kilda is all about glitzy nightlife. In Elwood, it’s all about the thriving café scene. neighborhood_overview:
The area: We are located on a quiet street which is no through road, tucked away behind the industrial side of Reservoir and bordering North Coburg. Our street ends at the Merri Creek, where you can find a rugged bush walking trail - follow this all the way to Coburg lake. Guarantee you won’t see anyone else ;)
Head the other way and you have industrial Newlands Rd with the bus, easy driving access to the city (12km down Sydney road, or drive/bus to catch the train), the Coburg Drive in (1km) Hawker Brewery (500m). We are a 1 km walk or quick drive to Edwardes Lake, running tracks, sports fields, the cafe and the best pizza in town (Off The Boat Pizzeria).
The new Pentridge Cinema and entertainment complex is just 3.5km down Newlands Rd, where you can also access Coburg Lake and the surrounding abundant parklands, including the Merri Creek bike and walking path.
A car will make your stay much more enjoyable and there is plenty of parking available.

name: Greeve is in the heart KING BED-KING ROOM-PARKING
description:
Fitzroy is about Adventure and Discovery.
Enjoy easy access to everything from this centrally located place.

Home is warm & welcoming if you want to relax..

Much to my surprise - I made it to Superhost , but what an adventure its been.

I've hosted people from around the world. Along with my experience after having lived with them and learning about their experiences Ive learned what it takes to create a space that works for everyone & people enjoy being in. Continuing to do so.

The space
Its a 2 to 3 bedroom property- Open plan plan living with a private courtyard - great for entertaining. Its been designed for easy living combining old and new. Heritage and Modern.
Tis the people who've stayed here that have helped turn it into the cool pad it is today , - not forgetting me ... Ive met some great people via hosting and guests have forged great friendships with people they've met here. Some keep in touch and visit
neighborhood_overview:
Fitzroy is young and hip. Its Melbournes first suburb.
Too much to do & / or see. .. If youre seeking adventure and love discovering or finding things out, there simply is no better place to be.
host_about:
Hi Reader, 
I hope you are well. 

As a note ,  I'm new to this site so Im still learning about its workings. Its a great prospect that I hope to learn much from , hosting, but also look forward to having the opportunity to give back.  
I love living in fitzroy, I grew up here and am sure you will too. 

Just one thing I need to touch on;
... by no means is the house brand spanking new. Its a terrace / cottage thats 150 years young, having undergone some improvents twenty plus years ago , then more recently with gas heating being put in , plus ceiling fans being introduced too.  There's a balance I think of old and new in the house. 
Much like myself ,  It has loads character, furthermore, the house is ready to welcome guests but for you to enjoy its suurounds fully, please understand it too comes with its quirks. 
Its a work in progress , and has a bit of a rustic feel to it too,  but has good bones. In winter when it rains you can hear it pelt down on the tin roof,  While open , nice and airy , some improvements are still needing to be had, but even as is, that wont come at the expense of its guests. Its comfy, clean & hygienic - has a homely feel I hope with each piece having a story. 
Keep in mind-  Being inner city , if doors and windows are left open, a critter may enter - keep this in mind -  fly , spider , mouse., bees , lizards, birds. Yes , with nature around us-  & with neighbors having pets it can attract them and us too. While critters are not pests to everything or everyone - we try to live in harmony with them-Of course if this makes you too queasy this probably is not the right place for you. 

I work hard at making sure each stay is better than the last with upgrades and changes undertaken between stays. 
It's taken me the better part of three years to get it to this standard , I can only imagine that in three more years time , it'll look & feel even nicer. Hosting mostly through other sites - 
For me , home can be about loved ones, sharing moments and creating new memories. It should be a place to relax, take in and enjoy. A place where you are free to be You. I hope you enjoy being here as much as I do - its the people that have stayed here that have helped make it what it is today- that can only improve with the years. I'm learning and noticing something new each day in the house so its overall a pretty exciting place to be in.  Please don't hesitate to reach out , if I can be of any further assistance-  I'll do whatever possible to ensure you get the best from this home wherever I can .  

Lastly & with the busyish life I lead , I still have not had a chance to update both listings accordingly. I promise I will get to it very soon though.  (Im talking about my guide books as well. ). 
As a option : If you wanted to get a better idea about the house itself  -  area included & from my perspective - check out both Guidebooks for Greeves St property . Together they contain more information that I hope you will finduseful, there's more to come. .. please be patient- I hope to create a book for the house that'll make life easier too.  Til then , tho, ...

As per mentioned, I will be / am available anytime for questions and should there be anything on your mind/s that'll help make your stay that extra little bit more enjoyable, you need only let me know. Requests are what I'm talking about too. If not sure.. Ask ..whats the worst thst can happen , I'll always try to find an answer , or solution, -  I love a bit of a challenge ,  - I understand also that by learning more about what my guests are looking for earlier, I can be better equipped to handle and take extra care of their needs too. 
This makes for an overall better place all round. 

Thank you for your time, imput & support.  Much love . X 

Warm regards, 
Greeves Street

Our couples guest studio is on a lifestyle property, in the Warburton valley, on 10 acres of private land. Note: NOT SET UP FOR INFANTS/TODDLERS. Relax in our parkland garden area or walk through our very own piece of the forest. Accommodation includes: Queen bed, shower and toilet, desk, TV, kitchenette with bar fridge, small microwave, kettle, toaster, coffee machine, (No cooking facilities). There is no WiFi. Ideally located for visits to some of the Yarra Valley's best known attractions.

The space
Our gravel driveway is shared with the barn studio. The barn is serviced by rain water tanks. Share our "Swing Chair Shelter" to read, sip a glass of wine and be in nature, or walk our forest tracks. Some of the experiences we offer in addition to the accommodation include: Professional Life Coaching, Short drives into the nearby Great Dividing Ranges, Mt Donna Buang, Redwood Forest, Rainforest Gallery, 4WD (modest) experiences. For costs please enquire via email
neighborhood_overview:
Warburton has been a family destination and tourist trail for many years. Our local tourist information centre and the official website "visitwarburton" enable you to start your experience even before you leave home.
An information folder of maps and notes are provided for your use, sharing local information of places to visit, eat and explore.
host_about:
We are Pauline and Joseph, enjoying a lifestyle property in the Yarra Valley for 20 years.
Our family and friends all over the world who come to stay with us experience 'Canta Libre' as a haven for its setting and surroundings. We invite you to enjoy  the semi rural life style happenings.

description:

Location wise, it's fantastic! Close to Chapel St precinct and St Kilda (Beach + Botanical Gardens) and Windsor/Prahran for the best shopping and public transport at your door step.
+ FREE UNLIMITED WIRELESS WIFI & PARKING
- STONG SMELLING FOODS EG. CURRY ARE PROHIBITED ON THE PREMISES.

The space

neighborhood_overview:
St Kilda is home to many of Melbourne's famous visitor attractions including Luna Park, the Esplanade Hotel, Acland Street, Botanical Gardens, and Fitzroy Street. It is home to St Kilda Beach, Melbourne's most famous beach, several renowned theatres and several of Melbourne's big events and festivals.

Windsor: Melbourne's famous Chapel Street shopping strip bisects Windsor, and the Windsor end is seen as being the more bohemian, less expensive end of the street, although as development continues, the Windsor end of Chapel Street is becoming highly sought-after real estate, with many of the smaller retro-type clothing and vintage stores.
host_about: A few years ago I was living the bachelor life but then by stroke of luck I meet my lovely partner Jane and before I knew it I had moved in with her, leaving my apartment. Jane says it took a bit of convincing for me to leave but I’m sure you will understand why during your stay. Nowadays I’m a father of 2 boys and I’ve decided to rent out my place on airbnb. I’ve listed my apartment so that I can help pay with childcare bills. Please check out my listing and if you have any questions please feel free to ask. I look forward to hearing from you. All the best David

name: The Junction
description:
The Junction tiny house is the perfect country getaway for fitness enthusiasts who like to take advantage of what the Yarra Valley has to offer. It is also for those who want to take in the sights and sounds of the country life and just relax and enjoy for what nature has to offer. There are so many options for whatever you choose to do. This area caters to all your required needs – just no hustle and bustle of city life.

Welcome to country living!

The space
Our tiny house is a lovely studio space of 155 sqft. It offers guests a comfortable stay with its well-equipped kitchenette, complete with a queen sized bed, ensuite bathroom, air-conditioning and heating. Perfect for 2 persons!

The kitchenette has everything you need to cook your light meals with. A portable cooker, cookware, cooking utensils, cutlery, dishes & silverware, microwave and even a mini fridge. The portable cooker can be kept in the drawers when not in use to maximize space
neighborhood_overview:
Bring your bike or hire one from Girt by Dirt in Yarra Junction. Take a ride or walk along the rail trail O‘Shannassay Aqueduct trail, the Californian Redwood Forest Cement Creek or ride to the top of Mount Donna Buang. You may also choose to bring your horse and ride the trail. There is a fully fenced horse paddock with water troughs available.

We are located in a quiet rural neighbourhood. You will feel the country vibe and hear sounds of bird life and farm animals around. You will also see mountains, Eucalyptus, and even the odd kangaroo.

Where to eat:
- Ride or walk the rail trail to Yarra Junction or Warburton for a delicious breakfast and great coffee. For the best local roasted coffee and coffee tastings, I recommend Silva’s coffee which is 1.5km away
- Wesburn is 3km away where you will find the Flying Apron Patisserie for everything delicious
- You can go to Millgrove Dairy for handmade cheese selection and right next door is Yarra Valley A
host_about: Tiny Away spearheads the tiny house eco-tourism in Australia. It started because of our desire to offer city dwellers the perfect place to escape from their hectic digital life in the city. Riding on the international trend in tiny houses, we have taken the tiny house movement one step further by partnering with land hosts and placing our beautifully designed and handcrafted tiny homes on wheels in spectacular rural settings. By integrating the concept of tiny houses with eco-tourism, we want to allow everyone a chance to experience the tiny house lifestyle - To Discover Nature and Stay in Comfort.

host_about:
Dear Guest,

My husband and I both own and manage a vineyard in the Yarra Valley. We grow and supply grapes to renowned Winemakers in the region. We both are Vine growers by passion and IT Engineers by profession. We love to be out in the Nature, explore countryside and enjoy meeting new people from different cultures.

We enjoy different style of food and love to explore local produce around the valley. This farm is our dream that we are building together and you are most welcome to enjoy it responsibly :)

COVID has brought us lot of uncertainty of Travel and hence we are following Flexible Cancellation Policy so that you get full refund 1 day prior to your arrival in case you had to cancel or reschedule your stay. 

We want our guests an enjoyable and memorable farm stay experience and we are happy to help to make that happen. Just let us know in case you need anything which is not listed here. We are just a phone call away for any queries or information regarding your stay.

During your stay

You will be able to access the whole farm. It is a working farm and we are trying to share with you a real-life farm experience. We hope you will respect and follow House Rules for everyone's safety and security during your stay. We are sure you will love sipping the local Wines while soaking in the breathtaking views... :)

neighborhood_overview:
Carlton is a lively suburb, well-known for the Italian cafes and restaurants of the Lygon Street Italian precinct. It is also known for its Victorian buildings and leafy parks and gardens.

Carlton is a diverse suburb. Growing beyond its largely Italian residents, these days Carlton has a greater proportion of residents from eastern Asia, south-eastern Asia and India.

It also has the second largest population of our municipality. Many of Carlton's residents are local and international students who attend the nearby University of Melbourne and RMIT University.

As well as entertainment, cultural and leisure activities, Carlton has educational, business, retail and residential facilities. Carlton Gardens contain the contemporary Melbourne Museum and the domed 19th century Royal Exhibition Building that has World Heritage status.

We specialise in offering a new and unique opportunity to overseas, inter and intrastate visitors, career professionals, academics, post and undergraduates, trainees, language students, doctors, teachers and other business specialists to gain superior inner Melbourne accommodation and lessen the stresses of moving to a new town, new state or a new country.

We offer studios and self contained options. As well as share houses where new arrivals, to Melbourne, or people going through a lifestyle transition, can experience an instant community atmosphere for short or long term stays, and say goodbye to lonely, restrictive and expensive apartment living.

No more solitary TV dinners! All properties provide apartment house accommodation on a room-by-room basis with shared kitchen, dining and lounge facilities. This style of apartment house living provides opportunities for new arrivals, to Melbourne to quickly, form friendships, build networks with other likeminded people and settle more easily into a new life. 

A safe and comfortable home life makes for a happier Melbourne experience.

We hope you choose to stay with us and we have the opportunity to make your stay in Melbourne a life long happy memory.

host_about:
Hey there you curious creatures! Time to share a little about ourselves. Mike was born and raised in New Zealand and I have a Greek background but am from a small country town in Victoria. Would you believe that in all the time we’ve been together, I haven’t been able to convince Mike to do the HAKA for me? And if you're wondering, the answer is yes...he’s an ex-rugby player and All Blacks man.

Mike trained as a chef and moved to the UK in his early 20’s to work and travel through Europe. He is a Sensei, 1st Dan black belt and passionate about teaching children the art of Karate. Mike is also a qualified landscaper, is now heavily involved in property and is one of those people who’s annoyingly good at everything.

I graduated and practiced as a Podiatrist for a couple of years, before moving into the advertising industry, security risk management and recently a furniture design & importing business. We launched our hosting journey as a way to help others create unforgettable experiences during their travels whilst having the freedom to personally explore more of our amazing world at the same time!

We’re both fans of entrepreneurship, love Gary Vaynerchuck (if you’ve never heard of him, do yourself a favour and look him up) and enjoy studying personal development, online business, the sharing economy and anything that piques our interest at the time. Podcasts, Kindle, books, online courses...we love learning new things.

Thanks for stopping by. We hope the read was worth it and so look forward connecting with you as hosts or as travellers. We will do our best to make your stay truly memorable in all the right ways and also can’t wait to be great guests if we happen to chance by your Airbnb in your little corner of the globe. 

One more thought to take with you wherever you may be...

We Travel Not To Escape Life, But For Life Not To Escape Us.

See you very soon!


Mike & Panagiota x

neighborhood_overview:
Saint Kilda East, Victoria, Australia
St Kilda is an inner suburb of the metropolitan area of Melbourne, Victoria, Australia, 6 km south-east of Melbourne's Central Business District. Its local government area is the City of Port Phillip. At the 2016 Census, St Kilda had a population of 20,230

St Kilda was named by Charles La Trobe, then superintendent of the Port Phillip District, after a schooner, Lady of St Kilda, which moored at the main beach for much of 1841, and the ship's master and early settler Lieutenant James Ross Lawrence.

During the Victorian era, St Kilda became a favoured suburb of Melbourne's elite, and many palatial mansions and grand terraces were constructed along its hills and waterfront. After the turn of the century, the St Kilda foreshore became Melbourne's favoured playground, with electric tram lines linking the suburbs to the seaside amusement rides, ballrooms, cinemas and cafes, and crowds flocked to St Kilda Beach. Welcome to the vibrant city of Melbourne! As your host in South Yarra, Victoria, I am excited to show you all that this incredible city has to offer. But my love for Melbourne extends beyond just its sights and sounds. As a foodie and avid sports fan, I am also happy to recommend the best places to grab a coffee, a craft beer, or catch a game. And as a lover of literature, art, and music, I am always up for a lively discussion on the city's vibrant arts scene.

I hope that your stay with me in South Yarra is more than just a place to rest your head. I want it to be an opportunity to fully experience all that Melbourne has to offer, and to make lasting memories in this beautiful city.

neighborhood_overview:
Fitzroy is an eclectic suburb with a rich history etched into its old pubs and gay bars, narrow streets and terrace houses, long-lasting retailers and modern wine bars that have kept the old decor. The suburb was once long associated with the working class and was known to have a bohemian vibe but became a casualty to gentrification in the ’80s and ’90s. Yet despite undergoing urban revitalisation, many heritage buildings and Victorian-era architecture have lasted through the decades and play a key role in the area’s ambience – right next to the street art.

You’ll find various characters within Fitzroy’s nooks, including many students, young professionals and those of the older generation that have called the community home for decades. It’s hard to pigeon hole the area but, after all, why would you want to?

host_about:
Hi Reader, 
I hope you are well. 

As a note ,  I'm new to this site so Im still learning about its workings. Its a great prospect that I hope to learn much from , hosting, but also look forward to having the opportunity to give back.  
I love living in fitzroy, I grew up here and am sure you will too. 

Just one thing I need to touch on;
... by no means is the house brand spanking new. Its a terrace / cottage thats 150 years young, having undergone some improvents twenty plus years ago , then more recently with gas heating being put in , plus ceiling fans being introduced too.  There's a balance I think of old and new in the house. 
Much like myself ,  It has loads character, furthermore, the house is ready to welcome guests but for you to enjoy its suurounds fully, please understand it too comes with its quirks. 
Its a work in progress , and has a bit of a rustic feel to it too,  but has good bones. In winter when it rains you can hear it pelt down on the tin roof,  While open , nice and airy , some improvements are still needing to be had, but even as is, that wont come at the expense of its guests. Its comfy, clean & hygienic - has a homely feel I hope with each piece having a story. 
Keep in mind-  Being inner city , if doors and windows are left open, a critter may enter - keep this in mind -  fly , spider , mouse., bees , lizards, birds. Yes , with nature around us-  & with neighbors having pets it can attract them and us too. While critters are not pests to everything or everyone - we try to live in harmony with them-Of course if this makes you too queasy this probably is not the right place for you. 

I work hard at making sure each stay is better than the last with upgrades and changes undertaken between stays. 
It's taken me the better part of three years to get it to this standard , I can only imagine that in three more years time , it'll look & feel even nicer. Hosting mostly through other sites - 
For me , home can be about loved ones, sharing moments and creating new memories. It should be a place to relax, take in and enjoy. A place where you are free to be You. I hope you enjoy being here as much as I do - its the people that have stayed here that have helped make it what it is today- that can only improve with the years. I'm learning and noticing something new each day in the house so its overall a pretty exciting place to be in.  Please don't hesitate to reach out , if I can be of any further assistance-  I'll do whatever possible to ensure you get the best from this home wherever I can .  

Lastly & with the busyish life I lead , I still have not had a chance to update both listings accordingly. I promise I will get to it very soon though.  (Im talking about my guide books as well. ). 
As a option : If you wanted to get a better idea about the house itself  -  area included & from my perspective - check out both Guidebooks for Greeves St property . Together they contain more information that I hope you will finduseful, there's more to come. .. please be patient- I hope to create a book for the house that'll make life easier too.  Til then , tho, ...

As per mentioned, I will be / am available anytime for questions and should there be anything on your mind/s that'll help make your stay that extra little bit more enjoyable, you need only let me know. Requests are what I'm talking about too. If not sure.. Ask ..whats the worst thst can happen , I'll always try to find an answer , or solution, -  I love a bit of a challenge ,  - I understand also that by learning more about what my guests are looking for earlier, I can be better equipped to handle and take extra care of their needs too. 
This makes for an overall better place all round. 

Thank you for your time, imput & support.  Much love . X 

Warm regards, 
Greeves Street

name: Peaceful 8 acre nature retreat
description:
Welcome to our piece of paradise! Our house stands on a one acre sloping field of grass surrounded by seven acres of towering gum and palm trees. The garden is a bird paradise, wallabies and wombats visit regularly, with only one neighbour in the distance.

Our house is surrounded by a covered verandah on three sides to enjoy the nature. The house has four bedrooms with garden views, two bathrooms, two living rooms, which comfortably fit eight people (ten on request).

The space
Our house is located on an 8 acre piece of native bushland. It is ringed by a towering forest and feels private and secluded, you just see a neighbouring house in the distance through the trees 100 metres away. At night the house is absolutely quiet, in the morning you might be awoken by the tweeting of birds or the laugh of a Kookaburra.

The house is luxuriously decorated with a contemporary feel, and quirky artwork throughout. But the real reason to come is nature,
neighborhood_overview:
The house is situated in the Dandenong ranges in a peaceful, lush valley. At the same time, it's only 2 minutes drive to the village Kallista, which has a small supermarket, and a few cafes and restaurants. It's five minutes drive to Olinda and Sassafras, two tourist-favorite mountain villages, and it's less than 10 minutes from Belgrave, with a large supermarket and all the amenities you might want.

There's a lot of amazing hiking around, and we are happy to share tips and tricks. For instance, Grant on Sherbrooke is two minutes drive from our home, and it is a protected nesting ground for lyrebirds with beautiful rainforest hikes.
host_about:
My husband an I are originally from the Netherlands and moved to Melbourne, Australia, for work purposes a few years ago. 
We recently bought a house out in the Dandenong ranges and absolutely love it here! Nature and wildlife are stunning, as if we are living in a nature reserve. We are very proud of our house and like to keep it in great condition 

We travel a lot for work and for fun, and in those times like to make our home available to other guests. When we travel we almost exclusively stay in Airbnb’s so we know exactly what makes a stay great and would like to give that experience to our guests as well.

host_about:
(Click on my logo above to see more than 20 other StayCentral listings).

I started StayCentral Melbourne Corporate Apartments with one apartment, and now manage multiple properties in many different locations in inner Melbourne.

Like many of my guests, I love to travel; both overseas to places like the Mulu Caves in Borneo, ancient cities (in many old countries), Antarctica & Alaska, Patagonia, and colourful markets in amazing places like Pha Long in Vietnam, Jaisalmer in India, Tozeur in Tunisia & La Paz in Bolivia. I also love short domestic trips for weekends away just to relax. 

It means I get to be on the other side of the counter, and experience how other people look after their guests: and I take their good ideas back with me. 

I even worked as a part-time tourist guide in Melbourne for a couple of years when I arrived here, and really enjoyed the interactions with the tourists off the huge cruise liners that appear in Melbourne each spring and summer. I have tried to extend this experience into my accommodation business here in Melbourne, where I really love assisting people with their stays. You only need to read some of the 1,000 plus reviews on Airbnb, Trip Advisor, Goo gle, and on my own StayCentral Melbourne Corporate Apartments website to realise how much our guests enjoy our apartments, and the way we look after them. 

I do love living in Melbourne. It enjoys a unique place in Australia. It is often described as the cultural, business, shopping and sporting capital of the country. The restaurants and cafes in Melbourne are amongst the best in the world. I love living in an urban village amongst these, and all the other everyday shops; and jumping on a tram and avoiding the hassles of traffic. It is a city of great opportunity, experiences, events and lifestyle that can be enjoyed by locals and visitors alike; and I really enjoy facilitating our guests' stays here.

neighborhood_overview:
Memories are built in this quiet little town in the end pocket of the Yarra Valley.

A hamlet surrounded by tall mountains with a fresh water river running through it. As one of Australia's (and the world's) best wine regions, people flock to myriad wineries dotted throughout the valley.

This quaint village sits at the end of the dividing range in Victoria, influenced by the bass straight weather patterns; it is lush year round with abundant wildlife and dense flora.

Plenty to take in:

- bushwalking; amazing walks surround the town....

- bike riding; ride the 40km historic Lilydale-Warburton rail trail. Looking for a more thrilling adventure, then scramble the mountain bike tracks. Bikes are available for hire at Cog Cafe. Just down the hill.

- in summer, the river is a fun place to be with swimming, kayaking or floating on a tube in the fresh water river.

description:
FitZroy Zen is a quiet, private apartment for one person or a couple located in the heart of everything that is quintessentially Melboune. We are on the edge of the CBD opposite the ornate Carlton Gardens, yet moments walk from the hippest inner city suburbs of Fitzroy, Collingwood and Carlton. You can walk everywhere you need to go.

The space
Located in a restored Heritage building opposite the Royal Exhibition centre, this is a modern fully renovated ground floor apartment with a contemporary Japanese twist. It includes a queen sized bedroom, fully equiped kitchen, dining table for 6, living room with Smart TV (Netflix, Stan and Binge) and sound bar.

There is a private out door terrace including a calming water feature and bamboo and, uniquely, FitZroy Zen includes a separate meditation room. This room can be used a massage room - please contact us if you are interested in renting FitZroy Zen for this purpose.

Double glazing, reverse cycle
neighborhood_overview:

Walk out the rear to Gertrude Street with its trendy cafes, restaurants and exclusive shopping.

We are next to the St Vincent's Hospital Complex.

2 min walk to Brunswick Street and the back streets of Fitzroy with historic pubs, eclectic International restaurants and bars.

5 min walk to the restraints, bars and LGBTI nightlife of Smith Street; voted Australia's 'Hippest Street' by ABC listeners.

You can walk everywhere from FitZroy Zen or get any tram on Nicholson Street into the city and beyond, including direct trams to St Kilda and Docklands. The free tram zone starts at Parliament Station, 400m walk.

This charming, one-of-a-kind regional Hotel established in 1885 has undergone a restoration of the 32 rooms of accommodation. Dine and beverage downstairs and enjoy country hospitality. Explore the beauty of the Warburton village alongside the Yarra river and at the of foot of Mount Donna Buang; stroll the old railway line, O'Shannassy aqueduct or magnificent Redwood forest and inhale the Alpine air. Room 19 caters for a family stay of four with a minimum two night stay with A/C & heating.

The space
Room 19 has a king bed and single bunk set with ensuite. The room overlooks the Rail trail at the back of the property.
There is a restored ensuite with locally made Warbotanical bodywash, shampoo and conditioner.
This family suite has a two night minimum stay on weekends and is very popular with A/C and heating.
It is recommended you book a table in the downstairs lounge where you will be warmed by the wood fire in winter or unwind in the beer garden o
host_about: Hi, I am one of the dedicated Alpine Hotel team members invested in bringing this grand gal of a regional Hotel to life in stunning Warburton, the hidden gem of the Yarra Valley.  We offer relaxed country hospitality, a seasonal bistro menu, great beverages, housemate pizzas only one & half hours from Melbourne CBD.

neighborhood_overview:
Discover the artsy part of Fitzroy where the alternative side of Melbourne is in full-force. Fitzroy is the inner-city mecca for all things cool and kooky. Wander along Brunswick Street, Fitzroy’s main drag, for lots of vintage clothing stores, street art, and second-hand bookshops. Take your pick from an array of cafés, bars, and restaurants, like the famous vegetarian spot Vegie Bar.

Fitzroy's locals are laid back and super friendly, and the entire area has a culturally diverse vibe. My apartment is centrally located and perfect for exploring the local area on foot, as well as other cutting edge Northside pockets of Melbourne such as Smith Street in Collingwood, Lygon Street in Carlton, and Bridge Road in Richmond.

Located 5 minutes away from the apartment, you'll find the Melbourne Museum, The IMAX Theatre, The Royal Exhibition Building, and the Carlton Gardens.

host_about:
Hey there you curious creatures! Time to share a little about ourselves. Mike was born and raised in New Zealand and I have a Greek background but am from a small country town in Victoria. Would you believe that in all the time we’ve been together, I haven’t been able to convince Mike to do the HAKA for me? And if you're wondering, the answer is yes...he’s an ex-rugby player and All Blacks man.

Mike trained as a chef and moved to the UK in his early 20’s to work and travel through Europe. He is a Sensei, 1st Dan black belt and passionate about teaching children the art of Karate. Mike is also a qualified landscaper, is now heavily involved in property and is one of those people who’s annoyingly good at everything.

I graduated and practiced as a Podiatrist for a couple of years, before moving into the advertising industry, security risk management and recently a furniture design & importing business. We launched our hosting journey as a way to help others create unforgettable experiences during their travels whilst having the freedom to personally explore more of our amazing world at the same time!

We’re both fans of entrepreneurship, love Gary Vaynerchuck (if you’ve never heard of him, do yourself a favour and look him up) and enjoy studying personal development, online business, the sharing economy and anything that piques our interest at the time. Podcasts, Kindle, books, online courses...we love learning new things.

Thanks for stopping by. We hope the read was worth it and so look forward connecting with you as hosts or as travellers. We will do our best to make your stay truly memorable in all the right ways and also can’t wait to be great guests if we happen to chance by your Airbnb in your little corner of the globe. 

One more thought to take with you wherever you may be...

We Travel Not To Escape Life, But For Life Not To Escape Us.

See you very soon!


Mike & Panagiota x

neighborhood_overview:
St Kilda is an inner suburb of the metropolitan area of Melbourne, Victoria, Australia, 6 km south-east of Melbourne's Central Business District. Its local government area is the City of Port Phillip. At the 2016 Census, St Kilda had a population of 20,230

St Kilda was named by Charles La Trobe, then superintendent of the Port Phillip District, after a schooner, Lady of St Kilda, which moored at the main beach for much of 1841, and the ship's master and early settler Lieutenant James Ross Lawrence.

During the Victorian era, St Kilda became a favoured suburb of Melbourne's elite, and many palatial mansions and grand terraces were constructed along its hills and waterfront. After the turn of the century, the St Kilda foreshore became Melbourne's favoured playground, with electric tram lines linking the suburbs to the seaside amusement rides, ballrooms, cinemas and cafes, and crowds flocked to St Kilda Beach. Many of the mansions and grand terraces became guest house

neighborhood_overview:
St Kilda is an inner suburb of the metropolitan area of Melbourne, Victoria, Australia, 6 km south-east of Melbourne's Central Business District. Its local government area is the City of Port Phillip. At the 2016 Census, St Kilda had a population of 20,230

St Kilda was named by Charles La Trobe, then superintendent of the Port Phillip District, after a schooner, Lady of St Kilda, which moored at the main beach for much of 1841, and the ship's master and early settler Lieutenant James Ross Lawrence.

During the Victorian era, St Kilda became a favoured suburb of Melbourne's elite, and many palatial mansions and grand terraces were constructed along its hills and waterfront. After the turn of the century, the St Kilda foreshore became Melbourne's favoured playground, with electric tram lines linking the suburbs to the seaside amusement rides, ballrooms, cinemas and cafes, and crowds flocked to St Kilda Beach. Many of the mansions and grand terraces became guest house

The property is strategically located 45 minutes drive from Melbourne CBD and Mornington; 5 minutes drive to Monash M1 and Princes highway. Near to Eden Rise shopping center at Berwick (Cafes, Aldi, Coles etc).

The town of Berwick was originally part of the Cardinia Creek run. Subdivision started in 1854 and a store, post office, hotel and other businesses were established. Wheat, barley and potatoes were grown, with a flour mill operating for several years. Dairy farming and cheese making later became the main activities. History comes alive in Berwick high street even today !

Food scene

Sightseeing

neighborhood_overview:
Leafy and abundant – Ivanhoe is a snapshot of suburban paradise. Renowned for its beautiful Art Deco architecture, here there are homes that have enough flair to stop you in your tracks.. Set on sprawling blocks, life in these stylish twentieth-century mansions feels a world away from the hustle of the CBD, belying this in-demand suburb’s prime location.

But it’s not just the architecture that draws visitors to Ivanhoe. Since the 1950s, Melburnians have been flocking to this charming neighbourhood to see the Christmas lights on display each year at The Boulevard. In keeping with this family-friendly feel, the neighbourhood streets are eternally green and peaceful.

Buzzing Upper Heidelberg Road, where neighbours come to meet for coffee and gossip, is where all the action in Ivanhoe takes place. host_about:
Hey there you curious creatures! Time to share a little about ourselves. Mike was born and raised in New Zealand and I have a Greek background but am from a small country town in Victoria. Would you believe that in all the time we’ve been together, I haven’t been able to convince Mike to do the HAKA for me? And if you're wondering, the answer is yes...he’s an ex-rugby player and All Blacks man.

Mike trained as a chef and moved to the UK in his early 20’s to work and travel through Europe. He is a Sensei, 1st Dan black belt and passionate about teaching children the art of Karate. Mike is also a qualified landscaper, is now heavily involved in property and is one of those people who’s annoyingly good at everything.

I graduated and practiced as a Podiatrist for a couple of years, before moving into the advertising industry, security risk management and recently a furniture design & importing business. We launched our hosting journey as a way to help others create unforgettable experiences during their travels whilst having the freedom to personally explore more of our amazing world at the same time!

We’re both fans of entrepreneurship, love Gary Vaynerchuck (if you’ve never heard of him, do yourself a favour and look him up) and enjoy studying personal development, online business, the sharing economy and anything that piques our interest at the time. Podcasts, Kindle, books, online courses...we love learning new things.

Thanks for stopping by. We hope the read was worth it and so look forward connecting with you as hosts or as travellers. We will do our best to make your stay truly memorable in all the right ways and also can’t wait to be great guests if we happen to chance by your Airbnb in your little corner of the globe. 

One more thought to take with you wherever you may be...

We Travel Not To Escape Life, But For Life Not To Escape Us.

See you very soon!


Mike & Panagiota x

neighborhood_overview:
The Yarra Valley is a visually inspiring and historical region with a passion for food and wine. It offers the pleasures of day spas, boutique art and craft, wineries, open gardens, a Chocolaterie, country style cafes and gourmet restaurants, as well as a variety of outdoor activities for both young and old. Glenlowren is just under an hour from Melbourne CBD and 60 minutes from Tullamarine airport.

The two closest Yarra Valley towns are Yarra Glen (10 minutes) and Healesville (12 minutes). Both have an eclectic range of eateries and many activities to choose from. In the cottage you will find a guest folder with suggestions for things to visit or do, like the Chocolaterie, Gulf Station, TarraWarra Museum, Healesville Sanctuary, walks, drives and of course the wineries.
host_about: Ten years ago, my husband and I decided to move from Melbourne to the Yarra Valley and we never looked back! The quiet and serene environment, we have found to be a draw card for many of our friends and visitors. We therefore decided to rebuild the dilapidated historic cottage into a striking bed and breakfast and self contained style accommodation, enabling us to open up the property and so share all its joys with those who want to take in and enjoy this type of setting.  We derive great pleasure from this

The Shack is ideal for couples attending events or weddings in the local ar
neighborhood_overview: Emerald is set in the Dandenong Ranges and an excellent place to explore the surrounds. With Puffing billy, Emerald Lake park, Cardinia Dam just a short drive away and Emerald township which offers some great cafes, restaurants and our famous Emerald Bakery. Within 10-15 minutes are the towns of Monbulk, Kallista, Sassafras, Cockatoo and Gembrook which all have wonderful places to eat, gardens, parks, walks and bike riding tracks. Within 15-20 minutes we have several wineries - Yarra Ranges Estate, Paternoster Winery, Emersleigh Estate and Carlei Estate.  Well worth a visit!

name: Charming cottage with the city on your doorstep
description:
Gorgeous single fronted terrace house in the heart of Fitzroy North. You will have everything you need on your doorstep including cafes and bakeries, yummy restaurants, bars and local pubs nestled in the surrounding back streets. It’s a 5 minute walk to multiple tram stops and a 10 minute ride into the city to access all the art, music, theatre, sport and cuisine Melbourne has to offer. It’s also a short stroll to the beautiful greenery of the Edinburgh gardens and Merri Creek.

The space
My house is light, comfortable, full of character and a little bit quirky. Enter through the green front door and you’ll find a cosy lounge room, wall mounted TV and green velvet couch that converts into a comfy sofa bed if needed. The main bedroom has a comfortable Queen bed, lots of wardrobe & drawer space and a remote control sky light that closes itself if it detects rain. The open kitchen/dining area has induction stove top, marble bench tops and Miele appliances, including
neighborhood_overview: The streets of Fitzroy North are peaceful, tree lined and filled with beautifully maintained heritage properties. There is a strong sense of community in the area and so many great options for eating and drinking in every direction. There’s a butcher, fishmonger, supermarket, bakery and health food shop all within walking distance if you want to stay home and cook. But why would you when there are so many delicious restaurants locally! I’m very happy to provide you with recommendations.
host_about: Hi, I’m Liz, I recently bought this gorgeous terrace house in Fitzroy North. I love love love this area of inner city Melbourne, there is so much to explore right on your doorstep. I love to travel and have used Air bnb more times than I can count. I much prefer it to a hotel because you get to immerse yourself in an area and live like a local. I have so many recommendations for you, please just ask. Enjoy your travels!

neighborhood_overview:
Healesville is a tourism mecca that goes back over 100 years as a holiday destination.

First and foremost, you've got to walk down to Four pillars gin distillery and have a gin paddle or cocktail. Four pillars gin have been awarded two years in a row, the best international gin maker in the world.

Enjoy the many local wineries on offer. There are 75 cellar doors in the Yarra Valley.
We highly recommend Helen and joeys estate, Payten and Jones, Boat O Craigo, Steels Gate wines, and Yileena Park wines.

The house is a 5 minute drive from the famous Healesville Sanctuary.

Enjoy the many local hikes from Maroondah Dam to Mt. St. Leonards.

The best convenient coffee and snacks are located next door at the BP service station (Browser Bean Cafe).

name: Hygge Hus in the heart of Warburton
description:
Your family and friends will be close to everything wonderful Warburton and surrounds has to offer in our centrally located home.

Nestled off the street with mountain views and sounds of the teeming Yarra to welcome you. The famous Warburton Trail is a stones throw away providing easy access to the local pub (5 min walk), the town centre (8 min walk) and Water World (12 min bike).

We are family friendly and can provide a portacot, bassinet and change table to make your stay easy!

Guest access
You can access and use the whole property during your stay!
neighborhood_overview:
With the Yarra River meandering through the town, lined with gorgeous trees and numerous parks, Warburton offers a relaxing experience. The Lilydale-Warburton Rail Trail either starts or ends here (depending on your direction), there are numerous hiking trails to choose from and kayaking on the river is a spectacular experience.

The Redwood Forrest is a must see in the area and Mount Donna Buang is a 22 minute driveaway offering more hiking and snow play (in winter) fun.

For those with young families, the newly build Warburton Water World is a fun place to spend the day!

host_about:
(Click on my logo above to see more than 20 other StayCentral listings).

I started StayCentral Melbourne Corporate Apartments with one apartment, and now manage multiple properties in many different locations in inner Melbourne.

Like many of my guests, I love to travel; both overseas to places like the Mulu Caves in Borneo, ancient cities (in many old countries), Antarctica & Alaska, Patagonia, and colourful markets in amazing places like Pha Long in Vietnam, Jaisalmer in India, Tozeur in Tunisia & La Paz in Bolivia. I also love short domestic trips for weekends away just to relax. 

It means I get to be on the other side of the counter, and experience how other people look after their guests: and I take their good ideas back with me. 

I even worked as a part-time tourist guide in Melbourne for a couple of years when I arrived here, and really enjoyed the interactions with the tourists off the huge cruise liners that appear in Melbourne each spring and summer. I have tried to extend this experience into my accommodation business here in Melbourne, where I really love assisting people with their stays. You only need to read some of the 1,000 plus reviews on Airbnb, Trip Advisor, Goo gle, and on my own StayCentral Melbourne Corporate Apartments website to realise how much our guests enjoy our apartments, and the way we look after them. 

I do love living in Melbourne. It enjoys a unique place in Australia. It is often described as the cultural, business, shopping and sporting capital of the country. The restaurants and cafes in Melbourne are amongst the best in the world. I love living in an urban village amongst these, and all the other everyday shops; and jumping on a tram and avoiding the hassles of traffic. It is a city of great opportunity, experiences, events and lifestyle that can be enjoyed by locals and visitors alike; and I really enjoy facilitating our guests' stays here.

I do hope I have the opportunity to help you get to know Melbourne and grow to love it as I have. If you have any questions about StayCentral Melbourne Corporate Apartments, please feel free to call me on (Phone number hidden by Airbnb) during business hours, and I will do my best to help you.

name: Off Chapel Retreat
description:
Moments from Chapel Street this peaceful and centrally located self contained unit is stylishly presented. A convenient, comfortable pad from which to enjoying the thriving neighbourhood of Windsor.
From a separate security entrance enjoy a shared courtyard and private open plan living area. Main bedroom features a queen size bed. Second bedroom has a double sofa bed or can be used as a study. While the sofa bed can accommodate 2 people, it’s more comfortable for 1 person.

The space

neighborhood_overview: Off Chapel Retreat is less than 5 minutes' walk to the bustling Windsor end of Chapel Street, filled with bars and great restaurants and plenty of great spots to grab coffee in the morning. neighborhood_overview:
ABOUT ALBERT PARK

Avalon is an Art Deco apartment block located in Albert Park which is less than 3 kilometers from Melbourne city.

Albert Park sits between Port Melbourne on Port Phillip Bay to the north and Middle Park with its stunning park and lake to the south. Nearby is the popular tourist destinations of the Melbourne city and St.Kilda.

Albert Park is famous for its well-preserved historic Victorian (late 19th Century) and Edwardian (early 20th Century) houses and its quiet, wide, tree lined streets.

Take a short walk and you can enjoy the outstanding heritage protected Victorian architecture of St.Vincents Place, sunsets over the bay at South Melbourne Beach, the fairy penguins at St.Kilda pier or watch the many sports being played in Albert Park.

Stroll down the many fascinating bluestone laneways and discover the remarkable renovations hidden behind the preserved heritage facades.

Nearby are many popular a
host_about:
YOUR HOST: GARY COPOLOV 

Hello, my name is Gary and I live in Middle Park, Melbourne.

I currently manage over twenty inner bay side short to long term accommodation properties located in Middle Park, Albert Park, South Melbourne, Port Melbourne, St.Kilda, Balaclava, Elwood and even one property in North Carlton and Torquay.

Those in the "hood" know that these locations are arguably some of Melbourne’s best and most desirable inner-city suburbs. I believe I am fortunate to live and manage properties in the area and to meet and host so many wonderful guests. 

My Airbnb hosting journey started when my wife, Jo and I bought Florence Court in Nimmo Street, Middle Park in 2015. It is a heritage listed, mid 30's Art Deco apartment block in where I now offer four fantastic short term accommodation apartments. I now operate sixteen brilliant short to long term accommodation properties in the area ranging from apartments to houses and from one to four bedrooms. They are all stunning and fitted out to the very highest standard. 

As an Airbnb Superhost I offer my guests a concierge like service. I am an unashamed ambassador for the area where I have lived for over 30 years. Just tell me your interests and I will offer my recommendations. 

Each property has a copy of "Adventures" which provides details my recommended local To Do's and the very best of nearby dining pleasures. There are many recommendations for guests of all ages. 

My wife, Jo and I met playing tennis at Kooyong Lawn Tennis Club and were married in Paris just over 28 years ago. Jo is a part time teacher at the local primary school where she is a special education reading teacher. She has a real passion for books, real estate and interior design. A typical week will see her at a local café with friends, walking our dog in the nearby park, seeing a movie, attending her book club, doing a yoga class or shopping for the next thing for one of the properties we fitout and list on Airbnb. 

We have two children, Louis and Natasha. Natasha (Tash) is a Project Co-ordinator in the construction industry. When she is not out socialising or getting away for the weekend, she may occasionally walk our friendly Nova Scotia Duck Tolling Retriever called Hudson. We love pets and welcome them at all properties. 

Our son Louis lives in Portland, Maine, USA where he works for the Boston Celtics basketball team as Player Manager of the G League Maine Celtics.  He attended university in Boston, USA and represented Australia in rowing as a coxswain. 

On weekends, given a chance, Jo and I visit a beach side get-away apartment with stunning sea views in Torquay, just over an hour’s drive from Melbourne. Torquay is Victoria's surf capital and the gateway to the famous Great Ocean Road. The apartment is available for stays on Airbnb. (Sleeps 8). In Torquay, we enjoy walks, great local food, wine, the many surf beaches and the company of friends. 

We have travelled to Europe, the Middle East, Southeast Asia and the USA where we have enjoyed Airbnb hospitality in England, France, Italy, Spain, Bulgaria, Croatia, Turkey, Netherlands, Denmark, Sweden and the United States. I wish to share that same pleasure and the excitement of travel with my guests. 

I am a self-employed Marketing Consultant whose consultancy, Gold Medal Marketing develops and implements winning business development programs for businesses in various industries. I have written and delivered a number of business development programs. 

When not hosting, I am working hard as the honorary Chairman of the Australian Patients Association (APA) which advocates, educates and supports Australian patients. The APA strives to be the voice of and for Australian patients. 

I regularly attend a local gym for regular workouts which I am pleased to introduce to my active guests. 

On weekends in Winter, I watch St. Kilda, an Australian Rules Football Club as a long standing and sadly long-suffering club supporter and member.

With a family of building designers, I am always working on some property development or creative project for myself, family or and friends or for the property owners that I represent. 

I like to catch a movie or show and visit galleries and believing that you should “be a tourist in your own town” try get out and about to something interesting either near or far. 

I look forward to sharing the many great properties which I manage and my local experience with my guests. 

I am always available to ensure that property owners are well served by my services and that guests have a great stay. 

Yours, 

Gary Copolov.

host_about:
MPORTANT: Dear potential guests,

Thanks for viewing our apartment/townhouse or house offering(s).  

It’s a very difficult and weird time to be running a essentially tourism based accomodation business, through COVID 19 on and off restrictions and lockdowns. 
It’s very stressful. We’ve had to lay off our administration support staff as we’re running a  low scale/skeleton version of our previous business.

Before enquiring please understand situation the following:

- most/all properties are currently on longer stays due to current landscape without normal tourism.  
- current long stay guests/tenants need to be contacted to double check if they are extending on or not. Some extend week by week or month by month.  But can’t block out future dates until they are financial.   Out of courtesy I need to give first right to current guests, as many guests are getting stranded in Victoria due to travel/covid restrictions.  Because of this availability and associated pricing is not always 100%  in real time.  If you are not happy for a manual avails check and quote, then respectfully please do not enquire.

FOR ALL ENQUIRIES and BOOKING REQUESTS:

- Please DO NOT request a booking, availability and pricing needs to be quoted manually at this time.  

Please use “Contact Host” button on listing with your enquiry (check in / check out and guest numbers and if any flexibility with your start/finish date). I will so my best to assist with your inner, bayside or Mornington peninsula accomodation needs.

Thanks for your respect and cooperation and I look forward to helping you with your accomodation needs

neighborhood_overview:
Our street is very quiet, all the houses in the area are owner occupied and it is very community focussed. On street parking is available. Untimed parking is available on cashmere st (100m away) and timed parking is available all other times; please check street signs carefully. Welcome to one of Melbourne’s top 5 most liveable suburbs.

We are 180m to tram stop 26 of route 59. It is a short 10 mins to the Victoria Market and 20 mins to Flinders St. Trams leave every 4-10 mins. We are a 10 min walk to Newmarket Train Station or Flemington Bridge Station which is 9 mins into the city.

The 2 cafes at the end of the street are excellent.

There is a mini supermarket, florist and wine bar at the end of the street. There is also the Laurel hotel and Dan Murphy’s a short walk away. The new metro supermarket is also a short walk away.

Lots of restaurants line Mt Alexander road toward Moonee Ponds. The junction and Puckle St is 2km away.

Find your slice of heaven next to our spring-fed pond, nestled in amongst Blackwood trees and just a stone’s throw from the mighty Mountain Ash of Sherbrooke Forest. Relax in the hammock, make friends with our animals, amaze at the abundant wildlife, or visit our stream amongst the giant tree ferns. Head out locally to explore the magical forest, picnic in the gorgeous Hills gardens, or take a memorable trip on Puffing Billy.

The space
The van is situated in the bottom half of our hill side property, which runs down into the valley. It is situated in the paddock, amongst Blackwood Trees and beside our spring-fed pond. You will be sharing the paddock with our alpacas Fred and Ginger, as well as our sheep. At the bottom of the property there is a small stream winding through giant tree ferns and Mountain Ash.

The van has a comfy double bed with quality linen, bedside tables a small wardro
neighborhood_overview: Van in the Valley is located in Selby in the Dandenong Ranges. neighborhood_overview:
Magnificent Melbourne has to be one of the best cities in the world and its food culture with an enormous array of dining and drinking establishments has to be experienced. The shopping is excellent as well. I love Melbourne and I can share lots of tips and recommendations.

Just a two-minute walk to the Port Melbourne beach with sand, sea, bike tracks, walking paths, and stunning scenery.

Just a three-minute walk to the iconic Bay St shopping strip. There are heaps of bars, pubs, restaurants, shops, boutique outlets, and lifestyle amenities. The variety is amazing and all within walking distance.

Just a 30-minute walk to Albert Park Lake which has wonderful scenery, gorgeous swans, you can hire a boat to sail, ride a bike around it or just simply sit and enjoy the parklands. neighborhood_overview:
Frankston is a suburb of Melbourne in Victoria, Australia, in the local government area of the City of Frankston. It is located 41 km south-east of the Melbourne city centre, north of the Mornington Peninsula. Due to its geographic location, it is often referred to as "the gateway to the Mornington Peninsula"

Situated on the eastern shoreline of Port Phillip, Frankston has been a popular seaside destination of Melbourne since the 1880s. Frankston Beach is still one of the most frequented in Victoria, and is recognised as one of the cleanest in Australia. It is also home to one of the largest exhibitions of sand sculpting in the Southern Hemisphere.

host_about:
MPORTANT: Dear potential guests,

Thanks for viewing our apartment/townhouse or house offering(s).  

It’s a very difficult and weird time to be running a essentially tourism based accomodation business, through COVID 19 on and off restrictions and lockdowns. 
It’s very stressful. We’ve had to lay off our administration support staff as we’re running a  low scale/skeleton version of our previous business.

Before enquiring please understand situation the following:

- most/all properties are currently on longer stays due to current landscape without normal tourism.  
- current long stay guests/tenants need to be contacted to double check if they are extending on or not. Some extend week by week or month by month.  But can’t block out future dates until they are financial.   Out of courtesy I need to give first right to current guests, as many guests are getting stranded in Victoria due to travel/covid restrictions.  Because of this availability and associated pricing is not always 100%  in real time.  If you are not happy for a manual avails check and quote, then respectfully please do not enquire.

neighborhood_overview:
In the words of Broadsheet: "Brunswick is Melbourne’s biggest centre of alternative culture. Walk the streets and you’ll spot more tattoos, piercings, lurid hair colours and op-shop clothes than anywhere else. Every night of the week, local bands play at venues such as Howler, the Brunswick Hotel, the Retreat and the Spotted Mallard. Vegan food is plentiful and everyone seems to ride a bike.

That’s one part of it, anyway. Brunswick also has a strong multi-cultural character. Greeks and Italians have been here since the ’60s and more recently, immigrants from Lebanon and other parts of the Middle East have clustered around the north end of Sydney Road, making it one of the best places in town to eat falafel; smoke a shisha pipe; or shop for ingredients such as za’atar, rosewater and Aleppo peppers.

Neighbouring Brunswick East has much of the same character, but swaps Sydney Road for the less-hectic end of Lygon Street and its cafes, restaurants and boutiques."

neighborhood_overview:
Brunswick East is a vibrant and welcoming neighbourhood. The suburb has an eclectic feel about it and nearby Lygon Street is a central hub of diverse cultural backgrounds.

Brunswick today is the home of hipsters and bohemians who fancy the alternative arts scene, but it’s the Greeks, Lebanese, Turks and Italians who inhabited the area in the 1960s are who we have to thank for a lot of the neighbourhood’s food and culture.

The suburb is easily accessible by public transport with two trams lines servicing the suburb making getting in and out of the city easy. It’s also east to get around by bicycle using the many flat bike paths.

The leafy Merri Creek Trail runs through Brunswick bounded by the Moonee Ponds Creek Trail.

Weringerong started life in 1910 as The Hospice Guesthouse and catered to convalescing returned servicemen. Today, it is a tastefully renovated home featuring a blend of contemporary and original features. Set in the heart of Olinda, restaurants and cafes are right next door and the Olinda Precinct - featuring a children's adventure playground and dog park - is 5 minutes walk away. Olinda is the centre of the Dandenong's and all its natural beauty. Ideal for couples or families all year round.

The space
Recently renovated, the house has all the modern comforts but strongly maintains its heritage feel. It is perfect if you are looking for a cosy and relaxing stay away with friends and family.

The queen rooms are very spacious and light filled with old fire places, large rugs and comfy beds and pillows.

Working from home? Not a problem. The study has a large desk, monitor and a variety of lap top connections for your use. High speed internet
neighborhood_overview:
Weringerong has restaurants, cafes and specialty stores right next door. Within short walking distance are the Olinda Precinct adventure playground and off-leash dog park, (featuring spectacular views over the Warburton Ranges and Yarra Valley), the Olinda swimming Pool, the National Rhododendron Gardens, Cloud Hill Nursery and Gardens and the Olinda Tearooms.

The townships of Sassafras, Monbulk and Mt Dandenong, with it's observatory, are 5 minutes drive. Sherbrooke Forest featuring walks through Mountain Ash and fern forests is close by and it is on these walks that you may see and hear Lyrebirds (Weringerong) on the forest floor. The historic railway "Puffing Billy" runs from Belgrave (10 minutes drive) to Gembrook with stops in Emerald and other places of interest. It is hugely popular with children and adults alike and tickets can be purchased on-line or at the Belgrave Station.

Road cycling and mountain bike riding are very popular in the hills. Many cl

name: Modern Studio with a View in Selby-Belgrave
description:
Located close to the Belgrave Sth shops, an easy drive to the Dandenongs' top attractions, and situated on 3.5 acres of native bushland and natural granite; this self-contained studio is a perfect base for couples and small families to experience all the hills have to offer. Enjoy the "toots" of Puffing Billy in the distance as you sip on a beverage while overlooking Menzies Creek, or take a walk down our private path to our little rainforest and discover the abundance of native flora and fauna.

The space
The studio is modern, cosy and comfortable. It has a split system AC, a small foldaway table, and basic kitchen amenities (microwave, toaster, kettle and sink). There's a double-bed and sofa bed located in the main open space, which would best suit a couple or family with 1-2 young kids. Enjoy the use of free fast wifi. There are also many DVDs and a chromecast available to stream your favourite shows. The studio is located approximately 30m from the main house
neighborhood_overview:
Selby is a rural area at the foothills of the Dandenong Ranges. You will love quiet walks around the streets exploring the neighbourhood, or discover a number of stunning walking tracks nearby. You will hear the "toots" of Puffing Billy in the distance and Selby Station is within walking distance if you'd like to catch a glimpse of Billy and give a wave to the tourists as they pass by.

We have an abundance of parks and picnic areas nearby including Birdsland, Cardinia Reservoir and the famous Grant's Picnic Ground all within a 10 minute drive of the property.

The Belgrave South Shops are a 20 minute walk or 2 minute drive away, offering everything you need with a Supermarket, Free-Range Butcher, Chemist, Natural/Whole Foods store, Cafe, Fish & Chips, Bakery and a great Bottle Shop offering local wines and an extensive craft beer selection.

Belgrave is a 5 minute drive away and is a bit more lively with plenty of bars, cafes,

host_about:
We proudly offer you a boutique selection of Rooms, Suites, Apartments and Cottages (many with water views) in beautiful, historic, Williamstown.

Rooms & Suites
Our lovely old home is just one door from the water, boat marina and restaurants and has been tastefully renovated and decorated so that every room has ensuites (some with spas) and most of the rooms also have gas log fires. One of the rooms has its own private lounge and balcony but other guests can enjoy the shared lounge room with fire and bay window.  

Apartments & Cottages
The apartments and cottages that we offer are dotted around Williamstown and most are an easy walk to the restaurants, yacht-clubs and shops. They vary from 1-4 bedrooms and 1-3 bathrooms. (One apartment even has a 4th toilet). 

We are not just offering a spare room in our house. This is our business and we are a dedicated team working to ensure that your stay is comfortable.  We are very careful about the apartments and cottages that we choose to take on and have refused many from owners wanting us to manage them.

Having said that, Williamstown is the oldest suburb in Melbourne and therefore some of the classic older apartments have décor such as shower over the bath. Whilst I would normally say no to venues like this, there are two apartments that have this but their other qualities so far outweigh that one negative and I quietly chip away at the owners in order for that to be changed.  I mention this because we want you to let us know if you have mobility issues so that we can suggest other venues for you. 

Other than beds (which are purchased to order and very comfortable!!), I love to buy antique and pre-loved furniture to fit certain venues and I have them restored using classic and sometimes quirky fabrics so you will not find a ‘hotel’ feel to our venues. Each one is quite different (some are Modern, some Victorian and some are Art-deco era). 

Re Dining: 

Breakfast: We provide a breakfast basket to guests staying in the apartments and cottages. Sadly due to restrictions related to covid, we do not currently offer breakfast in the main house so have reduced prices accordingly and our house-guests enjoy very good breakfasts in one of our many local cafes / restaurants, all within 2-4 minutes of here. (There are three places that we particularly recommend for a great breakfast).   There is however a table set in the bay window and you are welcome to use (Email hidden by Airbnb) plementary Tea, coffee and biscuits are provided in all venues. 

Dinner: There is an excellent choice of restaurants with a variety of cuisines all within walking distance to nearly all of the venues that we offer. 

I'm proud to say that we have won the last 7 awards for 'tourism' and/or 'hospitality' at the Hobsons Bay region Business excellence awards as well as taken out the People's Choice award for the state of Victoria twice in the last three awards. 

Most of our guests are businesspeople or travellers so you can be assured of a quiet, relaxing and private 'home award from home' experience. We want your accommodation to be comfortable and check-ins and check-outs easy so that you can get on with enjoying the reason that you are here.

We look forward to welcoming you to beautiful Williamstown. 

Best regards, 

Melissa and the team from Captains Retreat

- Walk in Robe;
- Revers
neighborhood_overview: Situated in a quiet and appealing tree lined street, filled with characteristic Victorian terraced and freestanding cottages, just two doors down from Blyth Street and a stones throw from the much celebrated Lygon Street and the Italian precinct. host_about:
We specialise in offering a new and unique opportunity to overseas, inter and intrastate visitors, career professionals, academics, post and undergraduates, trainees, language students, doctors, teachers and other business specialists to gain superior inner Melbourne accommodation and lessen the stresses of moving to a new town, new state or a new country.

We offer fully furnished, centrally located properties with plenty of parking and quality fixtures and furnishings, each has an alfresco entertainment area and BBQ  a fully equipped property in an exclusive professional apartment or house environment.

We offer studios and self contained options. As well as share houses where new arrivals, to Melbourne, or people going through a lifestyle transition, can experience an instant community atmosphere for short or long term stays, and say goodbye to lonely, restrictive and expensive apartment living.

No more solitary TV dinners! All properties provide apartment house accommodation on a room-by-room basis with shared kitchen, dining and lounge facilities. This style of apartment house living provides opportunities for new arrivals, to Melbourne to quickly, form friendships, build networks with other likeminded people and settle more easily into a new life. 

A safe and comfortable home life makes for a happier Melbourne experience.

We hope you choose to stay with us and we have the opportunity to make your stay in Melbourne a life long happy memory.

neighborhood_overview:
GET TO KNOW THE AREA
An industrial past, a multicultural feel and a sleek new urban scene make Richmond a true study in contrasts. While gentrification has been underway for quite some time, this inner-city suburb certainly hasn’t lost its vibrant charm. Chic bars, brunch spots and restaurants sit side by side with old-school pubs and music venues, making for one of Melbourne’s most eclectic neighbourhoods.

LIFESTYLE
There’s certainly no pigeonholing Richmond. Whether you’ve come to dine, dance, shop or don your footy scarf and cheer from the stadium (or the bar stool), this vibrant inner city suburb will keep you entertained endlessly.

While you can still see traces of its working-class roots in the grittier corners, Richmond has long since evolved into a multicultural ode to urban living. In parts sleek, in others a little more rough and ready, its streets are home to the glamour set, sports fanatics, hordes of trendy young professionals and well-estab
