host_about:
YOUR HOST: GARY COPOLOV 

Hello, my name is Gary and I live in Middle Park, Melbourne.

I currently manage over twenty inner bay side short to long term accommodation properties located in Middle Park, Albert Park, South Melbourne, Port Melbourne, St.Kilda, Balaclava, Elwood and even one property in North Carlton and Torquay.

Those in the "hood" know that these locations are arguably some of Melbourne’s best and most desirable inner-city suburbs. I believe I am fortunate to live and manage properties in the area and to meet and host so many wonderful guests. 

My Airbnb hosting journey started when my wife, Jo and I bought Florence Court in Nimmo Street, Middle Park in 2015. It is a heritage listed, mid 30's Art Deco apartment block in where I now offer four fantastic short term accommodation apartments. I now operate sixteen brilliant short to long term accommodation properties in the area ranging from apartments to houses and from one to four bedrooms. They are all stunning and fitted out to the very highest standard. 

As an Airbnb Superhost I offer my guests a concierge like service. I am an unashamed ambassador for the area where I have lived for over 30 years. Just tell me your interests and I will offer my recommendations. 

Each property has a copy of "Adventures" which provides details my recommended local To Do's and the very best of nearby dining pleasures. There are many recommendations for guests of all ages. 

My wife, Jo and I met playing tennis at Kooyong Lawn Tennis Club and were married in Paris just over 28 years ago. Jo is a part time teacher at the local primary school where she is a special education reading teacher. She has a real passion for books, real estate and interior design. A typical week will see her at a local café with friends, walking our dog in the nearby park, seeing a movie, attending her book club, doing a yoga class or shopping for the next thing for one of the properties we fitout and list on Airbnb. 

We have two children, Louis and Natasha. Natasha (Tash) is a Project Co-ordinator in the construction industry. When she is not out socialising or getting away for the weekend, she may occasionally walk our friendly Nova Scotia Duck Tolling Retriever called Hudson. We love pets and welcome them at all properties. 

Our son Louis lives in Portland, Maine, USA where he works for the Boston Celtics basketball team as Player Manager of the G League Maine Celtics.  He attended university in Boston, USA and represented Australia in rowing as a coxswain. 

On weekends, given a chance, Jo and I visit a beach side get-away apartment with stunning sea views in Torquay, just over an hour’s drive from Melbourne. Torquay is Victoria's surf capital and the gateway to the famous Great Ocean Road. The apartment is available for stays on Airbnb. (Sleeps 8). In Torquay, we enjoy walks, great local food, wine, the many surf beaches and the company of friends. 

We have travelled to Europe, the Middle East, Southeast Asia and the USA where we have enjoyed Airbnb hospitality in England, France, Italy, Spain, Bulgaria, Croatia, Turkey, Netherlands, Denmark, Sweden and the United States. I wish to share that same pleasure and the excitement of travel with my guests. 

I am a self-employed Marketing Consultant whose consultancy, Gold Medal Marketing develops and implements winning business development programs for businesses in various industries. I have written and delivered a number of business development programs. 

When not hosting, I am working hard as the honorary Chairman of the Australian Patients Association (APA) which advocates, educates and supports Australian patients. The APA strives to be the voice of and for Australian patients. 

I regularly attend a local gym for regular workouts which I am pleased to introduce to my active guests. 

On weekends in Winter, I watch St. Kilda, an Australian Rules Football Club as a long standing and sadly long-suffering club supporter and member.

With a family of building designers, I am always working on some property development or creative project for myself, family or and friends or for the property owners that I represent. 

I like to catch a movie or show and visit galleries and believing that you should “be a tourist in your own town” try get out and about to something interesting either near or far. 

I look forward to sharing the many great properties which I manage and my local experience with my guests. 

I am always available to ensure that property owners are well served by my services and that guests have a great stay. 

Yours, 

Gary Copolov.

Packed to the brim with buzzing cafés, bars, restaurants and clubs, there’s no place for boredom in this vibrant inner-city community.

Whether it’s catching a show at an independent theatre, clubbing until the early hours (or all hours if you so desire), or drinking and dining at one of the city’s hottest new venues, when it comes to revelry Prahran certainly doesn’t disappoint. This lively neighbourhood has the character and the stamina to keep you busy from day to night, to day again.

But rest assured it’s not all about debauchery. Prahran also offers a sophisticated dining scene and leafy residential backstreets. Between the shopping mecca of Chapel Street and the low-key cool of Greville Street with its record stores and boutiques, friendly Prahran still manages to pos

host_about:
We proudly offer you a boutique selection of Rooms, Suites, Apartments and Cottages (many with water views) in beautiful, historic, Williamstown.

Rooms & Suites
Our lovely old home is just one door from the water, boat marina and restaurants and has been tastefully renovated and decorated so that every room has ensuites (some with spas) and most of the rooms also have gas log fires. One of the rooms has its own private lounge and balcony but other guests can enjoy the shared lounge room with fire and bay window.  

Apartments & Cottages
The apartments and cottages that we offer are dotted around Williamstown and most are an easy walk to the restaurants, yacht-clubs and shops. They vary from 1-4 bedrooms and 1-3 bathrooms. (One apartment even has a 4th toilet). 

We are not just offering a spare room in our house. This is our business and we are a dedicated team working to ensure that your stay is comfortable.  We are very careful about the apartments and cottages that we choose to take on and have refused many from owners wanting us to manage them.

Having said that, Williamstown is the oldest suburb in Melbourne and therefore some of the classic older apartments have décor such as shower over the bath. Whilst I would normally say no to venues like this, there are two apartments that have this but their other qualities so far outweigh that one negative and I quietly chip away at the owners in order for that to be changed.  I mention this because we want you to let us know if you have mobility issues so that we can suggest other venues for you. 

Other than beds (which are purchased to order and very comfortable!!), I love to buy antique and pre-loved furniture to fit certain venues and I have them restored using classic and sometimes quirky fabrics so you will not find a ‘hotel’ feel to our venues. Each one is quite different (some are Modern, some Victorian and some are Art-deco era). 

Re Dining: 

Breakfast: We provide a breakfast basket to guests staying in the apartments and cottages. Sadly due to restrictions related to covid, we do not currently offer breakfast in the main house so have reduced prices accordingly and our house-guests enjoy very good breakfasts in one of our many local cafes / restaurants, all within 2-4 minutes of here. (There are three places that we particularly recommend for a great breakfast).   There is however a table set in the bay window and you are welcome to use (Email hidden by Airbnb) plementary Tea, coffee and biscuits are provided in all venues. 

Dinner: There is an excellent choice of restaurants with a variety of cuisines all within walking distance to nearly all of the venues that we offer. 

I'm proud to say that we have won the last 7 awards for 'tourism' and/or 'hospitality' at the Hobsons Bay region Business excellence awards as well as taken out the People's Choice award for the state of Victoria twice in the last three awards. 

Most of our guests are businesspeople or travellers so you can be assured of a quiet, relaxing and private 'home award from home' experience. We want your accommodation to be comfortable and check-ins and check-outs easy so that you can get on with enjoying the reason that you are here.

We look forward to welcoming you to beautiful Williamstown. 

Best regards, 

Melissa and the team from Captains Retreat

host_about:
Hi from Bronnie and Neil ;-)
We love being airbnb hosts and sharing our homes and our beautiful city with others.
We have been airbnb hosts since 2013 and during that time we have met thousands of amazing and wonderful people from all around the world and Australia.
We offer a range of properties, from high end luxury to family friendly. We also love to share our large and comfortable Guest House (Rosebank) in Healesville. 
We use airbnb ourselves when we travel and know that people are looking for - great locations at a good price, that are clean, comfortable and friendly! We like to think we offer all of that and more! All our are properties are professionally cleaned and we use hotel quality, commercially laundered linen on our comfortable beds.  We try to meet all our guests on arrival to say hello and are on hand to offer help, advice and inside tips of places to go and things to see. 
Most of our properties include free parking which is a real bonus in the city and Foxtel (cable) television and other homely touches so you 'can live like a local'. (You can view our properties by clicking the View Full Profile link below and clicking on the listings on the left hand side of the page). 

On a personal note, we're pretty easy going, and love getting out and about exploring different places. We are both keen travellers and have done our fair share of it in Australia and overseas - whether backpacking, camping, or an occasional splurge on 5-star resorts! You wouldn't call us 'party animals', but we do like socialising and meeting people.  We enjoy a wide variety of interests, love the movies, eating out, popping down to the beach or river, and enjoying the many exciting sporting events and festivals that Melbourne and regional Victoria has to offer.
Our motto is "strangers are friends you haven't met yet!"
Wishing you happy travels and looking forward to meeting you soon :-)

Best regards
Bronnie and Neil

The space

Off the dining area is the updated kitchen with everything you need to cook up a storm. Other modern touches at The Cottage include ducted heating, reverse cycle heating and cooling, Nespresso coffee machine (pod style) and of course a dishwasher.
Sleeping up to 9 guests, The Cottage has four bedrooms, including two queen beds, two sets of single bunk beds and a king single, all with comfortable bedding for a good night’s sleep.

name: The Little Sanctuary - Altona
description:
Prepare to unwind and relax the moment you step foot inside our home.

The natural light, minimal styling, generous sized rooms and polished timber floorboards make "The Little Sanctuary" the ideal space to disconnect, spend time with a loved one or enjoy pure peace and quiet.

The space
Spend a day relaxing in "The Retreat", a sun filled lounge full of books, outside in the courtyard or curled up on the comfy high back chair.

A lazy 400m stroll takes you to Altona Beach, one of the nicest beaches Melbourne has to offer so slow down and take in the fresh salty air.

Meet a loved one and dine at one of Altona's many restaurants, a 2 minute drive from The Little Sanctuary or jump on the train and be in Melbourne's CBD within 20 minutes.

The Little Sanctuary is quiet, as are our neighbours. With only 3 other units on the block, you won't even know anyone is around. (But do feel free to say hello to those you meet, we'r
neighborhood_overview:
You are just 400m from Altona Beach, home to many recreational and water sport activities such as kite surfing, paddle boarding and volleyball. The calm, clean water leads to a pristine expanse of sand ideal for soaking up the sun, having a picnic or building a sandcastle.
A pathway along The Esplanade stretches the length of the beach. Shaded by lush Norfolk Pines, the shared bike and walking trail is part of the Hobsons Bay Coastal Trail and provides the ideal spot to enjoy the fresh air. Altona Beach is also well-known for its pier. The Altona Pier stretches 500 metres out over the water offering amazing views out to Port Phillip Bay.

Further, 1.2km walk or a 2 minute drive, you will find Pier Street - your retail hot-spot. Here you will find specialty, destination stores and supermarkets. a With over 25 cafes & restaurants, there is an abundance of appetising places to eat, drink and relax - all with stunning views down to Altona Beach.

neighborhood_overview:

host_about:
We specialise in offering a new and unique opportunity to overseas, inter and intrastate visitors, career professionals, academics, post and undergraduates, trainees, language students, doctors, teachers and other business specialists to gain superior inner Melbourne accommodation and lessen the stresses of moving to a new town, new state or a new country.

We offer studios and self contained options. As well as share houses where new arrivals, to Melbourne, or people going through a lifestyle transition, can experience an instant community atmosphere for short or long term stays, and say goodbye to lonely, restrictive and expensive apartment living.

No more solitary TV dinners! All properties provide apartment house accommodation on a room-by-room basis with shared kitchen, dining and lounge facilities. This style of apartment house living provides opportunities for new arrivals, to Melbourne to quickly, form friendships, build networks with other likeminded people and settle more easily into a new life. 

A safe and comfortable home life makes for a happier Melbourne experience.

We hope you choose to stay with us and we have the opportunity to make your stay in Melbourne a life long happy memory.

name: Vintage Alphington shopfront... 'Patricia's Place'
description:
If you're looking for 5 star luxury, and marble bathrooms...then 'Patricia' isn't the place for you!
Dive into the history of this unique and memorable shopfront.
My mum 'Patricia' was pretty quirky, and so is this whimsical space.
100 years old, and an Alphington institution...this adorable shopfront was once a lolly shop, and is one of the areas most loved buildings.
The interior is warm and spacious, with plenty of room to rest or work, and everything you need for a comfortable stay..

The space
This adorable vintage shop is a large open plan space with everything you need for a comfortable stay...quality queen bed, crisp linen and fresh fluffy towels.
Quirky light fittings and lamps providing a cosy atmosphere.
Large comfy leather sofa with large screen and AppleTV.
A full kitchen with microwave, stove, and dishwasher.
There's a vintage pink dining table with comfy velvet chairs and retro armchairs to relax in.
A v
neighborhood_overview:
Alphington is a beautiful leafy neighbourhood, just 10 km from Melbourne CBD
Patricia's Place is located on a quiet residential street, just meters from the railway station...20mins on the train to Flinders St.
Easy walking distance to several cafe's, restaurants, Dan Murphy's...and the local Farmers Market on Sundays!
Darebin Parklands and the Yarra River are a short distance away...a very enjoyable bike ride!

I am a professional photographer, working mainly with tiny newborn babies!

neighborhood_overview:
Our home is in the heart of East Kew surrounded by parks, schools, and cafés.
Public transport is just a stone’s throw away.
There are many nearby supermarkets that stay open until late and for gourmet provisions - Toscano’s in Kew and Bocaccio’s Cellars in North Balwyn are world-class.

host_about:

I manage multiple properties in Melbourne and nationally. I love the fact that my work let's me meet so many interesting guests and hosts. I'm an avid traveler and I enjoy using Airbnb whenever I visit a new country.
My love of nature and the outdoors sees me diving, hiking, biking and exploring this amazing planet every chance I get ! I love good food and coffee and I'm always planning my next adventure :)

name: Light-filled Apt with 270° Views + Large Balcony
description:
Sunny 2BD/2bath apt in the heart of Collingwood. You'll be spoilt for choice with the city's best cafe's, bars, & shops at your doorstep. Tram stop just 20m from the front door so getting around is quick and easy.

Equipped with modern appliances, a spacious kitchen, study nook, balcony and views for days! Large windows throughout property - wake up to the sun and go to sleep with the city lights.

Take a short stroll to Edinburgh gardens or pop out to some of Melbourne's best coffee roasters.

The space
Amenities Overview:

- Unlimited NBN WiFi
- Apple TV

- Dishwasher

neighborhood_overview:
Collingwood is a bustling, eclectic inner-city suburb north of the CBD with endless options for trendy cafes, restaurants, pubs, bars, nightlife, boutique shops, vintage and second-hand stores, and more.

There is much to explore in Collingwood and the neighbouring suburbs of Fitzroy and Abbotsford which are also known for their artsy, eclectic vibe. If it's greenery you're after head over to the nearby Fitzroy gardens to enjoy an afternoon in the sun.

Close proximity to the CBD makes Collingwood the ideal base to explore all Melbourne as to offer - the 86 tram will take you straight to the city in less than 15-minutes but is also easily walkable if you'd like to take in the sights on foot.

description:
Whilst enjoying proximity to Albert Park Lake, the beach and CBD, Ferrars Place is perfect for couples or small families seeking an unforgettable urban escape. You’ll find you have all the time in the world to enjoy the outdoor lifestyle on the oasis terrace and even find a quiet moment to yourself in the floor to ceiling window dining area.

The space
With an abundance of natural light and refined design, Ferrars will have you feeling at ease from the moment you arrive. The renovated Victorian terrace weaves old and new in a synergy that speaks to a harmonious vibe with cultural texture with modern comforts. The mosaic of colourful art against fresh white and timber flooring embodies the story of Melbourne style.
Downstairs is dedicated to living and entertaining and it’s where you’ll find the long open plan living, dining and kitchen plus spacious outdoor area.

The living area bathes in light and connects to the outdoors through the large patio

description:
Live your best life with the whole family at this peaceful home. Centrally located a short walk to Tennyson Street shops & Ormond Road village, Ripponlea Train Station, Nepean Hwy trams … or take a stroll along the beautiful Elwood canal path to the beach. This light and bright renovated period home boasts 3 bedrooms (all BIR) plus large 4th bedroom/study/formal lounge. Spacious main bathroom plus separate powder room. Front and rear gardens for kids to play! *Contact for long stay discounts*

The space
Three large bedrooms at the front of the house, a smaller bedroom/study, large light-filled family bathroom, neat powder room, and the whole house opens up along the corridor to a large, cathedral ceiling open plan living/dining/kitchen with butler’s pantry. This enormous space looks out to a wall of greenery - the peaceful backyard.

Guest access
All of the house and garden is yours.

description:
This Luxuriously house is located in a quiet street in Glen Iris. This spacious, two-storey house contains a fully equipped kitchen, great-sized living area, big-screen cinema, gym, and a pool table upstairs. The swimming pool brings more fun for you, especially in the summertime. It also holds four bedrooms, two bathrooms, one of them is ensuite in the master bedroom. The space

A open, well-appointed kitchen with dinning table, quality cooking appliances complements an expansive family area designed to capture sunlight and flowing to a covered alfresco dining area, solar heated pool in a private garden.

Whilst upstairs the spacious master bedroom complete with ensuite and walking in robes. Two further bedrooms with b
neighborhood_overview:
Tucked away in Melbourne’s inner east, Glen Iris offers a village-like atmosphere conveniently close to the city. Abundant with plantlife, it’s hard to miss this suburb’s connection with the natural world. Sprawling over undulating hills, there’s pretty parks, leafy cycling routes and walking trails, all canopied by established trees. Attracting families and professionals young and old, you’ll find a diverse community looking for a slower pace of life with the space and cafe culture to enjoy it. Spend your weekends at the local swimming pool – topped off by a delicious meal at one of the many quirky eateries – or pack a picnic and head to gorgeous Gardiners Creek. Well-connected by train, roads and bicycle paths, you’re never too far away from the city if you need it.

description:

neighborhood_overview:
This luxury apartment is a stone's throw from Crown Casino's Entertainment Complex, Yarra River, Melbourne's CBD, MCG, Albert Park and Southbank's Arts Precinct. If you'd like to know more, keep reading!

WHAT MAKES SOUTHBANK A GREAT DESTINATION?

Southbank fronts the southern bank of the Yarra River, just opposite Melbourne's central business district.

Southbank has 2 km of absolute Yarra River frontage which is lined by wide pedestrian promenades offering scenic views of the river and city skyline. At the suburb's eastern riverfront end is Princes Bridge which leads directly to Melbourne's CBD.

Adjacent to the bridge is Arts Centre Melbourne which includes a concert hall and several theatres dominated by a distinctive white spire which soars into the sky.

Heading westwards along Southbank Promenade is the multi-level Southgate shopping centre which features a host of retail outlets, restaurants and cafes that overlook the river. <
host_about:
Hey there you curious creatures! Time to share a little about ourselves. Mike was born and raised in New Zealand and I have a Greek background but am from a small country town in Victoria. Would you believe that in all the time we’ve been together, I haven’t been able to convince Mike to do the HAKA for me? And if you're wondering, the answer is yes...he’s an ex-rugby player and All Blacks man.

Mike trained as a chef and moved to the UK in his early 20’s to work and travel through Europe. He is a Sensei, 1st Dan black belt and passionate about teaching children the art of Karate. Mike is also a qualified landscaper, is now heavily involved in property and is one of those people who’s annoyingly good at everything.

I graduated and practiced as a Podiatrist for a couple of years, before moving into the advertising industry, security risk management and recently a furniture design & importing business. We launched our hosting journey as a way to help others create unforgettable experiences during their travels whilst having the freedom to personally explore more of our amazing world at the same time!

We’re both fans of entrepreneurship, love Gary Vaynerchuck (if you’ve never heard of him, do yourself a favour and look him up) and enjoy studying personal development, online business, the sharing economy and anything that piques our interest at the time. Podcasts, Kindle, books, online courses...we love learning new things.

Thanks for stopping by. We hope the read was worth it and so look forward connecting with you as hosts or as travellers. We will do our best to make your stay truly memorable in all the right ways and also can’t wait to be great guests if we happen to chance by your Airbnb in your little corner of the globe. 

One more thought to take with you wherever you may be...

We Travel Not To Escape Life, But For Life Not To Escape Us.

See you very soon!


Mike & Panagiota x

neighborhood_overview: There is a multitude of things to do in the nearby area, with many wineries, restaurants, beautiful country drives as well as attractions like the Healesville Sanctuary or a picnic in the nearby Maroondah Reservoir Park
host_about:
Hi from Bronnie and Neil ;-)
We love being airbnb hosts and sharing our homes and our beautiful city with others.
We have been airbnb hosts since 2013 and during that time we have met thousands of amazing and wonderful people from all around the world and Australia.
We offer a range of properties, from high end luxury to family friendly. We also love to share our large and comfortable Guest House (Rosebank) in Healesville. 
We use airbnb ourselves when we travel and know that people are looking for - great locations at a good price, that are clean, comfortable and friendly! We like to think we offer all of that and more! All our are properties are professionally cleaned and we use hotel quality, commercially laundered linen on our comfortable beds.  We try to meet all our guests on arrival to say hello and are on hand to offer help, advice and inside tips of places to go and things to see. 
Most of our properties include free parking which is a real bonus in the city and Foxtel (cable) television and other homely touches so you 'can live like a local'. (You can view our properties by clicking the View Full Profile link below and clicking on the listings on the left hand side of the page). 

On a personal note, we're pretty easy going, and love getting out and about exploring different places. We are both keen travellers and have done our fair share of it in Australia and overseas - whether backpacking, camping, or an occasional splurge on 5-star resorts! You wouldn't call us 'party animals', but we do like socialising and meeting people.  We enjoy a wide variety of interests, love the movies, eating out, popping down to the beach or river, and enjoying the many exciting sporting events and festivals that Melbourne and regional Victoria has to offer.
Our motto is "strangers are friends you haven't met yet!"
Wishing you happy travels and looking forward to meeting you soon :-)

Best regards
Bronnie and Neil

neighborhood_overview:
All the attractions of Chinatown, located between Swanston and Spring streets on Little Bourke Street, are right at your doorstep. You'll discover plenty of eateries, shopping, and entertainment throughout this iconic and historic enclave.

Bourke Street Mall is a few minutes' walk, where you will find department and chain stores, specialty retailers, and lively buskers lining the street. Other local attractions include QV Square, IMAX Theatre, National Gallery of Victoria, Melbourne Museum, Fitzroy Gardens, and the State Library.

host_about:
We proudly offer you a boutique selection of Rooms, Suites, Apartments and Cottages (many with water views) in beautiful, historic, Williamstown.

Rooms & Suites
Our lovely old home is just one door from the water, boat marina and restaurants and has been tastefully renovated and decorated so that every room has ensuites (some with spas) and most of the rooms also have gas log fires. One of the rooms has its own private lounge and balcony but other guests can enjoy the shared lounge room with fire and bay window.  

Apartments & Cottages
The apartments and cottages that we offer are dotted around Williamstown and most are an easy walk to the restaurants, yacht-clubs and shops. They vary from 1-4 bedrooms and 1-3 bathrooms. (One apartment even has a 4th toilet). 

We are not just offering a spare room in our house. This is our business and we are a dedicated team working to ensure that your stay is comfortable.  We are very careful about the apartments and cottages that we choose to take on and have refused many from owners wanting us to manage them.

Having said that, Williamstown is the oldest suburb in Melbourne and therefore some of the classic older apartments have décor such as shower over the bath. Whilst I would normally say no to venues like this, there are two apartments that have this but their other qualities so far outweigh that one negative and I quietly chip away at the owners in order for that to be changed.  I mention this because we want you to let us know if you have mobility issues so that we can suggest other venues for you. 

Other than beds (which are purchased to order and very comfortable!!), I love to buy antique and pre-loved furniture to fit certain venues and I have them restored using classic and sometimes quirky fabrics so you will not find a ‘hotel’ feel to our venues. Each one is quite different (some are Modern, some Victorian and some are Art-deco era). 

Re Dining: 

Breakfast: We provide a breakfast basket to guests staying in the apartments and cottages. Sadly due to restrictions related to covid, we do not currently offer breakfast in the main house so have reduced prices accordingly and our house-guests enjoy very good breakfasts in one of our many local cafes / restaurants, all within 2-4 minutes of here. (There are three places that we particularly recommend for a great breakfast).   There is however a table set in the bay window and you are welcome to use (Email hidden by Airbnb) plementary Tea, coffee and biscuits are provided in all venues. 

Dinner: There is an excellent choice of restaurants with a variety of cuisines all within walking distance to nearly all of the venues that we offer. 

I'm proud to say that we have won the last 7 awards for 'tourism' and/or 'hospitality' at the Hobsons Bay region Business excellence awards as well as taken out the People's Choice award for the state of Victoria twice in the last three awards. 

Most of our guests are businesspeople or travellers so you can be assured of a quiet, relaxing and private 'home award from home' experience. We want your accommodation to be comfortable and check-ins and check-outs easy so that you can get on with enjoying the reason that you are here.

We look forward to welcoming you to beautiful Williamstown. 

Best regards, 

Melissa and the team from Captains Retreat

neighborhood_overview:
Fitzroy is an eclectic suburb with a rich history etched into its old pubs and gay bars, narrow streets and terrace houses, long-lasting retailers and modern wine bars that have kept the old decor. The suburb was once long associated with the working class and was known to have a bohemian vibe but became a casualty to gentrification in the ’80s and ’90s. Yet despite undergoing urban revitalisation, many heritage buildings and Victorian-era architecture have lasted through the decades and play a key role in the area’s ambience – right next to the street art. 

You’ll find various characters within Fitzroy’s nooks, including many students, young professionals and those of the older generation that have called the community home for decades. It’s economically diverse too, having one of Melbourne’s largest public housing complexes while also putting up some of the most exxy rent in all of the city. It’s hard to pigeon hole the area but, after all, why would you want to?

I am happy to give out any advice I can on Melbourne and further travel destinations in Australia. Please just give me a call during your stay or email me with any questions :)

host_about:
Hi from Bronnie and Neil ;-)
We love being airbnb hosts and sharing our homes and our beautiful city with others.
We have been airbnb hosts since 2013 and during that time we have met thousands of amazing and wonderful people from all around the world and Australia.
We offer a range of properties, from high end luxury to family friendly. We also love to share our large and comfortable Guest House (Rosebank) in Healesville. 
We use airbnb ourselves when we travel and know that people are looking for - great locations at a good price, that are clean, comfortable and friendly! We like to think we offer all of that and more! All our are properties are professionally cleaned and we use hotel quality, commercially laundered linen on our comfortable beds.  We try to meet all our guests on arrival to say hello and are on hand to offer help, advice and inside tips of places to go and things to see. 
Most of our properties include free parking which is a real bonus in the city and Foxtel (cable) television and other homely touches so you 'can live like a local'. (You can view our properties by clicking the View Full Profile link below and clicking on the listings on the left hand side of the page). 

On a personal note, we're pretty easy going, and love getting out and about exploring different places. We are both keen travellers and have done our fair share of it in Australia and overseas - whether backpacking, camping, or an occasional splurge on 5-star resorts! You wouldn't call us 'party animals', but we do like socialising and meeting people.  We enjoy a wide variety of interests, love the movies, eating out, popping down to the beach or river, and enjoying the many exciting sporting events and festivals that Melbourne and regional Victoria has to offer.
Our motto is "strangers are friends you haven't met yet!"
Wishing you happy travels and looking forward to meeting you soon :-)

Best regards
Bronnie and Neil

host_about:
We specialise in offering a new and unique opportunity to overseas, inter and intrastate visitors, career professionals, academics, post and undergraduates, trainees, language students, doctors, teachers and other business specialists to gain superior inner Melbourne accommodation and lessen the stresses of moving to a new town, new state or a new country.

We offer fully furnished, centrally located properties with plenty of parking and quality fixtures and furnishings, each has an alfresco entertainment area and BBQ  a fully equipped property in an exclusive professional apartment or house environment.

We offer studios and self contained options. As well as share houses where new arrivals, to Melbourne, or people going through a lifestyle transition, can experience an instant community atmosphere for short or long term stays, and say goodbye to lonely, restrictive and expensive apartment living.

No more solitary TV dinners! All properties provide apartment house accommodation on a room-by-room basis with shared kitchen, dining and lounge facilities. This style of apartment house living provides opportunities for new arrivals, to Melbourne to quickly, form friendships, build networks with other likeminded people and settle more easily into a new life. 

A safe and comfortable home life makes for a happier Melbourne experience.

We hope you choose to stay with us and we have the opportunity to make your stay in Melbourne a life long happy memory.

host_about:
I have lived in Melbourne for over 20 years and love the culture, food and people here. Yummy food is something I definitely cannot live without. I love all sort of food including Modern Australian, Asian, European etc. One of my favourite places for food is Koornang Road in Carnegie where I can eat a variety of different cuisines. My favourite travel destination is Cuba because the place is charming and the people are friendly. 

I have been to many places in Asia, Europe and South/Central America, and look forward to travelling more as things open!

My latest travelled destination was Cairns and I have been there twice. I love the lifestyle, the many outdoor activities, and the natural tropical and leafy atmosphere. I highly recommend those who have not been to Cairns to go and take a look!

While I do like my city life I also love what nature has to offer.

If I was travelling I would like the place to be homey and provide all the essentials like home. I hope you will like my place as much as I do, and most importantly stay safe and have fun!
